HeroBanner.name: Davit Lursmanashvili
HeroBanner.intro2: a passionate software developer specializing in crafting high-quality web experiences.
HeroBanner.intro3: I also just like to build useful stuff.

SkillsSection.title: Three working lanes, one product mindset.
SkillsSection.description: I work across interface implementation, data-connected architecture, and the tooling that keeps releases fast and dependable.

frontend.title: Build Interfaces
frontend.description: Product UI, responsive implementation, and design-aware front-end delivery.

data.title: Connect State & Services
data.description: State management, data fetching, and back-end foundations that keep products cohesive.

delivery.title: Ship With Reliable Tooling
delivery.description: Platforms, workflows, and language coverage that support delivery and collaboration.

ExperienceSection.title: Selected product work, arranged as stories you can open.
ExperienceSection.description: A continuously moving strip of recent project directions. Hover a card to pause the flow, then open it for the fuller context.

commerceReplatform.title: Commerce Replatform
commerceReplatform.subtitle: A front-end overhaul for a multi-market storefront, focused on conversion lift, reusable sections, and calmer editorial publishing flows.
commerceReplatform.description: I reworked the browsing and checkout surfaces around reusable UI primitives, clearer content slots, and steadier state transitions. The goal was to make launches easier to coordinate, keep localization predictable, and reduce the friction that appears when merchandising changes move faster than the codebase.

operationsWorkbench.title: Operations Workbench
operationsWorkbench.subtitle: A task-heavy internal suite for operational teams that needed faster triage, mobile continuity, and more confidence around live state.
operationsWorkbench.description: This project centered on dense workflows: assignment queues, status updates, field-ready views, and data feedback loops that had to stay understandable under pressure. I focused on giving the interface a clearer rhythm, reducing context loss between surfaces, and making the system feel dependable even when several people were acting on the same records.

servicePortal.title: Service Portal
servicePortal.subtitle: A customer-facing portal refresh that balanced self-service clarity, modular content, and a cleaner route into support moments.
servicePortal.description: The work here was about turning fragmented flows into one coherent product surface. I helped shape reusable building blocks, tightened the information hierarchy, and made the portal easier to extend without introducing visual drift across new sections and account-management paths.
